feat: strengthen friction instrumentation in formatAgentContext (#315) (#317)

* fix: auto-attribute katakaId from run.json in kime/maki record (#256)

When agents run with KATA_RUN_ID set, `kata kime record` and
`kata maki record` now automatically populate `katakaId` from
`run.json` when no explicit `--kataka` flag is given. The explicit
flag still overrides. `kansatsu record` already had this behaviour;
this brings kime and maki into parity.

- Add `katakaId` optional field to `DecisionEntrySchema`
- Add `katakaId` optional field to `ArtifactIndexEntrySchema`
- Add `--kataka <id>` option to `kata decision record`
- Add `--kataka <id>` option to `kata artifact record`
- Read `katakaId` from `readRun()` return value (no extra file read)
- 3 new tests for each command (auto-populate, override, absent)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: strengthen friction instrumentation in formatAgentContext (#315)

Agents under cognitive load defer friction recording to final summary
prose rather than making structured kansatsu calls. This caused 0
friction records in Keiko 6 despite real events (rate limits, lint-
staged reverts, Zod gotchas, worktree path failures).

Changes to formatAgentContext():
- Add --cwd <kataDir> to all record commands so agents can copy-paste
  from any working directory without path errors
- Add urgency block: "FRICTION — record immediately, before continuing"
  with active language and rationale (mid-run signal vs prose noise)
- Add concrete friction example with the full runnable command
- Clarify friction taxonomy label as "required for friction type"
- Add pre-reporting checklist in "When you're done": prompts agents to
  review for unrecorded rate limits, unexpected behavior, workarounds,
  anything that took more than one try

Also fixes pre-existing test failure in observation.test.ts where the
count assertion still said 5 after agent-override was added as the 6th
FrictionTaxonomy value.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
feat: Sandbox Code Execution + Hybrid Vector Memory (v2026.6.2)

Sandbox Mode (Programmatic Tool Calling):
- New code_exec tool: LLM writes Python/JS, executes in isolated Docker container
- Secure HTTP tool bridge: container calls TITAN tools via session-token auth
- Auto-generated Python stubs from tool registry (denied: shell, exec, process)
- Docker security: --cap-drop=ALL, --read-only, memory/CPU limits, no-new-privileges
- Inspired by Anthropic's PTC pattern, works with all 21 providers

Hybrid Vector Memory (Tier 2):
- Local semantic search via Ollama embeddings (nomic-embed-text)
- Hybrid search: keyword (BM25-style scoring) + vector cosine similarity
- Auto-indexes memories and graph episodes to vector store
- Falls back gracefully when Ollama/embeddings unavailable
- Config: memory.vectorSearchEnabled, memory.embeddingModel
- Zero external dependencies, zero API costs

Files: 88 tools, 3,225 tests (85 files), all passing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

  alabiliyordu — bu açık kapatıldı.

commit.message:
Add email registration step to SiteGate after access code

Two-step gate: code → register email → unlock.
- "Register your access" framing feels required; skip is tiny + muted
  ("Already registered? continue") so it reads as a returning-member escape,
  not a bypass
- Submits silently to new Netlify member_signup form (email + access_code)
- Stores cf_email_done + cf_member_email in localStorage; skip also sets flag
  so overlay never reappears
- Existing users with cf_access but no cf_email_done go straight to register
  step on next visit — retroactively captures email from current users

commit.message:
fix: autopilot loop never exits — assistant messages block auto-exit, loop_exit not handled

Two bugs caused the autopilot to cycle 25+ iterations instead of exiting:

1. getUnacknowledgedMessages returned assistant messages (from send_message
   and run_direct) which blocked the exit condition (pendingMessages === 0).
   The LLM would acknowledge them, send another message, creating a new
   unacknowledged assistant message — infinite cycle. Fix: filter by
   role != 'assistant'.

2. The LLM kept choosing "loop_exit" as its action, but only "pause" and
   "complete" were handled. loop_exit fell through to default/unknown_action,
   triggering reflections and continuing the loop. Fix: accept loop_exit as
   alias for complete.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Classify VectoredWithPackedDimensionOk → §6.7.1 [skip ci]

The test validates that a net declaration with the vectored keyword and one packed dimension is legal. The net_declaration syntax in §6.7 (footnote 16) states that when vectored or scalared is used, there shall be at least one packed dimension. §6.7.1 covers net declarations with built-in net types, which is the only production in the net_declaration grammar that includes the vectored|scalared keyword.

commit.message:
Fix CryptoKey not set when loading from sessionStorage in DB mode

The auto-restore flow loaded data from sessionStorage without first
restoring the CryptoKey from IDB, leaving _cryptoKey null. Save then
failed with "must be an instance of CryptoKey". Fix: always restore
CryptoKey from IDB before trying sessionStorage. Also add test encrypt
in restoreFromCachedKey to verify the key survived IDB round-trip.

* feat: add HarnessElementary, HarnessOIDCMiddleware, HarnessDatabaseService library targets

HarnessElementary:
- HTMLResponseBodyWriter: HTMLStreamWriter
- HTML+Hummingbird response extension
Extracted from identical copies in NLF/Web and NeonLaw/Web

HarnessOIDCMiddleware:
- AuthenticatedUser, OIDCIDTokenPayload
- AuthenticatedRequestContext / AuthorizedRequestContext protocols
- OIDCAuthMiddleware<Context>, AuthorizationMiddleware<Context>
- buildJWTKeyCollection(), OIDCConfigError
Extracted from Sagebrush/Web; fixes #if canImport(FoundationNetworking)
violation (removed entirely — not needed for cross-platform support)

HarnessDatabaseService:
- DatabaseService actor: Postgres (URL) and SQLite initializers
- migrate() runs HarnessDALConfiguration.migrations
- healthCheck(), shutdown()
Extracted from Sagebrush/Web DatabaseService; normalises the pattern
used inline in NLF/Web and NeonLaw/Web

Package.swift:
- New packages: hummingbird, hummingbird-lambda, elementary, jwt-kit,
  fluent-postgres-driver, swift-nio
- New products: HarnessElementary, HarnessOIDCMiddleware,
  HarnessDatabaseService

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: update Package.resolved for new dependencies

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add public init to OIDCIDTokenPayload

The Swift-generated memberwise initializer is internal and cannot be
used by consumers of the library. Add an explicit public init.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: replace URLSession with AsyncHTTPClient in buildJWTKeyCollection

URLSession.shared is unavailable on Linux. Switch to AsyncHTTPClient
which is cross-platform. Also remove unused hummingbird-lambda dependency
which pulled in swift-configuration 1.2.0, whose Data.bytes API is
unavailable in Apple Foundation on macOS.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: pin swift-configuration to 1.1.0 to fix macOS build

Version 1.2.0 introduced a use of `Data.bytes` that does not exist on
macOS, causing the `tests (macos-latest)` CI job to fail with:
"value of type 'Data' has no member 'bytes'".

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: constrain swift-configuration to <1.2.0 in Package.swift

Version 1.2.0 of swift-configuration introduced a use of `Data.bytes`
that does not compile on macOS, breaking the macos-latest CI job.
Adding an explicit .upToNextMinor(from: "1.1.0") constraint ensures
SwiftPM cannot resolve above 1.1.x even on a fresh cache miss.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: update CI to macos-26; revert swift-configuration pin

hummingbird transitively depends on swift-configuration, which uses
Data.bytes (RawSpan) — a macOS 26+ API. Local dev runs macOS 26 but
CI was on macos-latest (macOS 15), causing the build to fail. Update
the matrix runner to macos-26 and restore swift-configuration to 1.2.0.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: correct bad find-and-replace in ASSIGNED_NOTATION_STATE_MACHINE.md

workquestionnaireResult → workflowResult (4 occurrences). The
alignment→workflow rename was incorrectly merged with flow→questionnaire,
producing the nonsense term workquestionnaire.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: add copyright notice to README

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(grafana): clickable links in chat panel (#35)

* fix: correct GitHub repo path, Loki gRPC timeouts, Grafana dataproxy timeouts

- Fix GITHUB_DEFAULT_REPO and GITHUB_BRAIN_REPO_URL from cocacolasante/aibrain → cocacolasante/Sentinel (fixes 404 on PR listing)
- Loki: extend grpc_server_max_connection_age to 2h + add frontend_worker backoff/retry config to handle GOAWAY gracefully
- Grafana: raise GF_DATAPROXY_TIMEOUT to 300s and add per-datasource queryTimeout for Prometheus, Loki, and PostgreSQL (fixes context canceled errors)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(grafana): replace LLM-based Slack reader with direct API endpoint

The Slack Channel Reader panel was routing through /api/v1/chat, relying
on the LLM to dispatch to slack_read on every 60s refresh — causing
repeated failures when intent classification timed out or returned no data.

- Add GET /api/v1/integrations/slack/messages?channel=<name>&limit=<n>
  that calls Slack conversations.history directly via AsyncWebClient
- Dashboard panel now fetches from this endpoint, showing user+text per
  message with colour-coded severity keywords
- Eliminates LLM round-trip, token burn, and retry loop entirely

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(brain): agentic tool-use loop + auto-fix hardening

Add Claude native tool-use loop to the dispatcher so Sentinel executes
multi-step tool calls in a single response instead of deferring with
"give me a moment to read those results".

- llm_router: add AGENTIC_TOOLS schemas (server_shell, task_read,
  sentry_read, task_create, task_update) and route_agentic() async method
  that loops up to 8 rounds resolving tool_use blocks before returning;
  filter empty-content messages from history to prevent 422s; skip retry
  on anthropic.BadRequestError in route()
- dispatcher: add _tool_executor() that delegates tool calls to the
  registered skill registry; wire process() to route_agentic() so the
  Slack "thinking..." state covers the entire multi-round loop
- tasks: catch anthropic.BadRequestError in _llm_execute_task() round
  loop and fail immediately with a clear note instead of retrying 3x

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(grafana): make links clickable in Sentinel chat panel

Add URL rendering to the chat panel's inlinemd() function:
- Markdown links [text](url) → <a target="_blank"> anchor
- Bare URLs (https://...) → clickable link with word-break
- Lookbehind prevents double-linking URLs already inside href attributes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
Rewrite student dashboard with token-based Moodle API integration

Replace browser-based Moodle interaction with proper API calls:

- Login: token input UI with link to Moodle Security Keys page,
  validates token via get_site_info(), caches via save_cached_token()
- Download: fetches assignment files via MoodleAPIClient.download_file()
  directly into per-assignment subdirectories (course_dir/<slug>/)
- Submit: uploads notebook via upload_file() + save_submission()
- Feedback: fetches grade/feedback inline via get_submission_status()
- Edit/Validate: unchanged (local operations)

Per-assignment directories use slug derived from name (e.g. "A1" from
"A1. Introduction to..."). All buttons wrapped in mo.ui.dictionary
returned as global variable so marimo fires on_change callbacks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix Qwen3.5 abliteration pipeline: timeout, thinking tags, repetition, stability

- Increase pipeline timeout from 45 to 90 minutes for Lukey03 multi-pass runs
- Add <think> tag support (Qwen3.5 format) to CoT stripping in refusal detection
- Strip untagged "Here's a thinking process..." prefixes from Qwen3.5 output
- Increase refusal test max_tokens from 128 to 512 to get past thinking blocks
- Add repetition_penalty=1.2 to coherence and refusal test generation
- Cap prompts at 2000 to prevent Metal/GPU crashes
- Free GPU memory every 25 prompts instead of 100
- Add crash recovery stderr tee and startup banner
- Fix get_layer_modules import path

- AI responds and reads it aloud automatically
- Modal shows response with play/stop speaking controls
- Gradient styling with sparkle icon

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(db): add SQLite persistence for radar snapshots

- backend/database.py — SQLAlchemy 2.0 Core module:
    * engine → data/radar_history.db (auto-creates data/ dir)
    * Table: radar_snapshots (id, timestamp, spot, gamma_flip, flip_type,
      call_wall, put_wall, net_gamma, dealer_mode, contracts_used)
    * init_db() — CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS on startup
    * save_snapshot(data) — INSERT on every /api/radar call; errors are
      logged and swallowed so a DB failure never breaks the API response
    * get_history(minutes) — SELECT for the last N minutes, oldest-first

- backend/server.py:
    * FastAPI lifespan calls init_db() on startup
    * get_radar() calls save_snapshot() after each successful calculation
    * New endpoint: GET /api/history?minutes=30 → HistoryResponse with
      count + list of SnapshotEntry (validated via Pydantic)
    * Version bumped 0.2.0 → 0.3.0

- backend/requirements.txt — added sqlalchemy
- data/.gitkeep — tracks the data/ directory in git
- .gitignore — excludes data/*.db (and *.db-shm / *.db-wal), plus
  __pycache__, .env, .venv, IDE dirs, .claude/

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
